name: EasyOCR
summary: EasyOCR is a deep learning-based OCR engine that combines CRAFT for text detection and CRNN for recognition, supporting over 80 languages without requiring language-specific training data. It provides a unified Python API that handles both detection and recognition in a single call.
use_when: Multi-language text detection in natural scene images, product labels, and street signs. Rapid prototyping without custom model training or complex preprocessing pipelines.
avoid_when: CPU-only environments processing high-resolution images in real time. Handwritten text recognition. Projects requiring fine-tuning on custom fonts or domain-specific vocabularies.
pros: Simple Python API with a single Reader class handling both text detection and recognition
Supports 80+ languages out of the box without additional language pack installation
GPU acceleration significantly speeds up inference on CUDA-enabled devices
Performs well on natural scene text with varied lighting, angles, and backgrounds
cons: Very slow on CPU-only machines, especially for high-resolution images
High memory consumption when processing large images or batch inference without resizing
Struggles with handwritten text and heavily stylized fonts not represented in training data
No public fine-tuning API for custom datasets or domain-specific vocabulary

key_hyperparams.name: gpu
key_hyperparams.note: Whether to use CUDA GPU acceleration. Set to 'true' for GPU inference, which is dramatically faster than CPU. Requires PyTorch with CUDA support.

key_hyperparams.name: lang_list
key_hyperparams.note: Language codes to recognize. Pass as a comma-separated string like 'en,ch_sim'. Each additional language increases model load time and memory usage.

key_hyperparams.name: detail
key_hyperparams.note: Level of detail in output. 0 returns only text strings. 1 returns bounding boxes, confidence scores, and text. Use 0 for simple extraction.

key_hyperparams.name: paragraph
key_hyperparams.note: Whether to group detected text boxes into paragraphs. Set to 'true' for document-like structured output, 'false' for raw line-by-line detection.

quick_start:
import easyocr

reader = easyocr.Reader(['en'], gpu=True)
result = reader.readtext('image.jpg', detail=1, paragraph=False)
for (bbox, text, conf) in result:
    print(f"{text} (confidence: {conf:.2f})")
